title: ProcurementContract v1
description: Machine-readable schema for a selected responder contract linked to a procurement question lifecycle.

contract/id.description: Stable identifier of the procurement contract.

question/id.description: Question lifecycle identifier to which the contract belongs.

room/id.description: Room or execution channel bound to the selected responder path.

selected-offer/id.description: Identifier of the offer chosen for contract formation.

asker/node-id.description: Node acting for the asking side of the contract as the routing or hosting identity.

asker/participant-id.description: Participation-role identity on whose behalf the asking side entered the contract.

asker/pod-user-id.description: Hosted-user identity when the contract was created on behalf of a later pod-backed client flow. This is additive to, not a replacement for, `asker/participant-id`.

responder/node-id.description: Node selected to fulfill the answer contract as the routing or hosting identity.

responder/participant-id.description: Participation-role identity selected to fulfill or lead the responder side of the contract.

payment/amount.description: Agreed payment amount in minor units. When `payment/currency = ORC`, the value uses ORC minor units with fixed scale `2`.

payment/currency.description: Currency or settlement unit symbol for the contract payment.

payer/account-ref.description: Optional payer-side canonical settlement owner reference. The role lives in the identifier prefix, so no separate `payer/kind` field is used. This becomes required for the `host-ledger` rail.

payee/account-ref.description: Optional payee-side canonical settlement owner reference. The role lives in the identifier prefix, so no separate `payee/kind` field is used. This becomes required for the `host-ledger` rail.

settlement/rail.description: Settlement rail chosen outside the protocol core.

deadline-at.description: Deadline by which the responder must deliver or the contract expires.

escrow/node-id.description: Supervisory node responsible for the host-ledger escrow path.

escrow/hold-ref.description: Reference to the host-ledger hold created for this contract.

escrow-policy/ref.description: Escrow policy in force for this host-ledger contract.

deadlines/work-by.description: Responder delivery deadline in the host-ledger timeout cascade. This SHOULD align with `deadline-at`.

deadlines/accept-by.description: Deadline by which the payer should acknowledge delivered work.

deadlines/dispute-by.description: Last moment for opening a formal dispute under the contract policy.

deadlines/auto-release.description: Moment when escrow may auto-release if contract conditions are satisfied and no dispute is open.

acceptance/answer-format.description: Expected answer format used for acceptance checks.

acceptance/arbiter-set.description: Arbiter identities required when arbiter confirmation is part of the contract.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the contract.

transport/encryption-mode.description: Expected transport-level privacy mode for the narrowed execution path.
